column1: # Skills to install (upstream repo, skill name, optional SkillRelPath, disabled flag)
# skillRelPath: leave empty for default "skills/<name>", or specify custom path
# disabled: "true" to park in skills._disabled/ with SKILL.md.disabled
# oz-skills (JZKK720 fork of orca/oz-skills). 14 active, 1 blocked by SkillSpector (webapp-testing: HIGH TM1).
# webapp-testing: upstream blocked by SkillSpector (HIGH TM1 — shell=True in scripts/with_server.py).
# Ported as a clean methodology-only skill (no bundled scripts) -> local/webapp-testing.
# idea-to-design: hand-authored clean port of obra/superpowers brainstorming.
# Upstream blocked by SkillSpector (tool parameter abuse in stop-server.sh visual companion).
# This port removes the browser server entirely; pure process methodology only.
# design-md-library: hand-authored wrapper skill (Option B). Indexes the VoltAgent/awesome-design-md
# fork mirror (74 DESIGN.md files, Google Stitch spec). Source lives at ~/dev/upstream/design-md-library/.
# Fork mirror: ~/dev/forks/JZKK720/awesome-design-md/. Not a direct upstream install.
# gstack-review is a hand-adapted port (MIT, from garrytan/gstack via JZKK720/gstack fork).
# Not a direct install: the upstream SKILL.md has a Claude-Code shell preamble that breaks
# under VS Code Copilot. The adapted version lives at ~/.agents/skills/gstack-review/
# with reference/checklist.md + reference/*.md copied verbatim from the fork's review/ dir.
# Re-create manually if missing; do not re-install from the fork (it would overwrite the port).
# agent loop design. All pass SkillSpector (LOW/SAFE). No MCP deps, plain SKILL.md.
# coupling. Passes SkillSpector (LOW/SAFE). Skill is at repo root <name>/SKILL.md
# (not under skills/), so relPath is just the folder name.
# ported as methodology-only. Upstream uses mcp__codex__codex, $ARGUMENTS, Bash(*),
# research-wiki, GPU pilots — all Claude-Code-specific. Ports strip those deps, keeping
# the core research methodology (landscape survey, idea generation, novelty check).
# All 3 ports pass SkillSpector (LOW/SAFE) and skills-ref validate.
